decks.notes: The key concept of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while efficiently drawing cards and generating card advantage. The deck aims to control the board through counterspells, removal spells, and card draw, ultimately winning the game through attrition. By utilizing cards like Force of Will, Force of Negation, and powerful draw spells such as Ancestral Recall and Dig Through Time, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent in a 1 vs 1 Vintage format.

This control deck focuses on using Gifts Ungiven to search for key combo pieces, utilizing counterspells and disruption to control the game until the combo is assembled. It includes powerful mana acceleration in the form of artifacts like Black Lotus and Moxen, as well as powerful draw and selection through cards like Ancestral Recall and Brainstorm. The inclusion of Underworld Breach allows for potential graveyard recursion and explosive turns. The goal is to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents through careful resource management and timely disruption.

This Vintage Aggro-MUD deck focuses on quickly developing a powerful board presence by efficiently using artifacts like The One Ring, Manifold Key, and Mishra's Workshop. The deck also aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Chalice of the Void and Karn, the Great Creator while generating value from cards such as Mystic Forge and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. With access to a variety of mana sources and a solid beatdown plan, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with its fast and aggressive gameplay style.

The deck focuses on utilizing Paradoxical Outcome and other various combo pieces to generate card advantage and mana acceleration in order to assemble a powerful game-winning combination of cards. It includes cards like Narset, Parter of Veils and Quantum Riddler to control the opponent's resources while stocking up on cards through Stock Up and other draw spells. With powerful artifacts like Tinker, Time Vault, and Karn, the Great Creator, the deck aims to quickly assemble a winning board state and overwhelm the opponent with a well-executed combo.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Coveted Jewel combo by utilizing fast mana sources like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith. The strategy involves using cards like Paradoxical Outcome and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain to generate card advantage and tutoring effects to find key pieces like The One Ring and Time Vault. By controlling the board with cards like Force of Will and Sink into Stupor, the deck can set up a lock on the game while gaining incremental advantages with the powerful artifact synergy provided by cards like Manifold Key and Urza's Saga.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with cards like Stinkweed Imp and Golgari Grave-Troll, while utilizing cards like Bazaar of Baghdad to discard cards and trigger abilities such as Dredge. By utilizing powerful graveyard recursion cards like Prized Amalgam and Ichorid, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a swarm of creatures and finish them off with cards like Creeping Chill. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Force of Negation and Mindbreak Trap to protect its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm and disrupt the opponent with a combination of aggressive artifact creatures and disruptive artifacts like Chalice of the Void, Trinisphere, and Sphere of Resistance. The deck also aims to generate value and card advantage through cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Mystic Forge, and Golos, Tireless Pilgrim. Crucible of Worlds and Wasteland provide land disruption, while Nettlecyst and Inventors' Fair offer additional utility. Overall, the deck focuses on early aggression and resource denial to secure a quick victory in a Vintage 1 vs 1 format.

This Vintage 1 vs 1 deck focuses on utilizing the Paradoxical Outcome combo to generate a large amount of mana and card advantage by returning artifacts to hand. The deck includes powerful mana acceleration cards like Black Lotus and Moxen, as well as key combo pieces such as Tolarian Academy, Time Vault, and Voltaic Key. The goal is to set up a board state that allows for multiple activations of Paradoxical Outcome, drawing into win conditions or disruption to control the game and secure victory.

The deck utilizes powerful artifacts and spells to assemble a controlling combo that generates card advantage and mana acceleration. By combining cards like Coveted Jewel, Paradoxical Outcome, and Tinker with fast mana sources like Mishra's Workshop and Grim Monolith, the deck aims to lock down the opponent's resources while rapidly amassing its own advantage. Tezzeret, the Great Creator and Karn, the Great Creator serve as versatile threats and toolbox options, allowing the deck to adapt to various situations and disrupt the opponent's game plan effectively.

The deck focuses on aggressive strategies with a strong emphasis on artifact creatures and disruption. It aims to lock down the opponent's resources with cards like Null Rod, Sphere of Resistance, and Trinisphere, while also applying pressure with cards like Stonecoil Serpent and Patchwork Automaton. The deck also has Crucible of Worlds and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base, allowing for a faster clock to close out the game. Additionally, the deck has access to powerful artifacts like Black Lotus and Moxen to accelerate its game plan.

The deck revolves around assembling the Coveted Jewel combo with cards like Phyrexian Metamorph and Paradoxical Outcome, while utilizing powerful artifacts like The One Ring, Time Vault, and Mana Crypt for additional advantage. It also includes disruptive elements such as Force of Will and Sink into Stupor to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to generate a powerful advantage through card draw and mana acceleration, ultimately overwhelming the opponent with a combination of card advantage and board presence.

This deck focuses on utilizing the Oath of Druids mechanic to cheat powerful creatures into play early in the game, giving you the advantage in controlling the battlefield. With a mix of fast mana acceleration, disruption spells, and key creature threats like Atraxa and Oko, you aim to disrupt your opponent's game plan while establishing your own dominance. Cards like Show and Tell and Vampiric Tutor help you dig for the pieces you need to further solidify your control over the game, making it difficult for your opponent to overcome your strategic advantage.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling powerful artifacts and using them to generate massive amounts of mana and card advantage. By utilizing cards like Tinker, Paradox Engine, and Coveted Jewel, the deck aims to create explosive combos and overwhelm the opponent with a flurry of spells and abilities. The key strategy is to efficiently ramp up mana production, manipulate the board state with powerful artifacts, and disrupt the opponent's game plan while building towards a game-winning combo.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents' strategies through counterspells, removal, and mana denial while using card advantage and recursion to maintain control over the game. It utilizes powerful cards such as Black Lotus, Ancestral Recall, and Timetwister to gain an early advantage and close out the game quickly. The inclusion of Lurrus of the Dream-Den as a companion provides additional value by allowing key cards to be recast from the graveyard. Overall, the deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and win through incremental advantage and resource management.

The deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana and drawing cards quickly through the use of artifacts like Moxen and Paradoxical Outcome. It aims to assemble powerful combos such as Tinker into Bolas's Citadel or using Tezzeret, Cruel Captain to generate value with artifacts. The main win condition is to generate a large storm count and finish the game with a powerful spell like Tendrils of Agony or The One Ring. The deck also includes disruptive elements such as Force of Will and Hullbreacher to protect its combo pieces.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent with hate cards like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and void mirror while being aggressive with creatures like Archon of Emeria and Clarion Conqueror. It aims to control the game by limiting the opponent's resources with cards like Wasteland and Strip Mine, while also ramping up its own mana with artifacts like Moxen and Ancient Tomb. The combination of hate cards and aggressive creatures creates a disruptive and fast-paced gameplay that aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their own strategy.
